spec_type_rationale: Upstream /discover; no related UI-primitive code exists yet (renderer is electron-vite boilerplate) — scaffolds a fresh primitives layer

findings.content: No test infrastructure exists; must pick a renderer test stack (Vitest + Testing Library) before the first feature needing coverage and record it in docs/architecture.md — this feature is that first feature.
Strict mode, no 'any'; type external input as 'unknown' and narrow. Icon name must be a typed string-literal union, not a bare string.
Renderer modules resolve via the @renderer alias; keep code in its process dir (src/renderer), no cross-process imports. Never import Node/electron in renderer.
Shared renderer state lives in a zustand store; never mutate zustand state outside its store actions. The Toast queue must be a zustand store.
Components PascalCase, hooks use-prefixed camelCase, stores Store-suffixed; functions one responsibility, extract past ~40 lines; SOLID/DRY/KISS.
Never swallow errors; handle both success and error paths of every fallible operation.
Three-process model; primitives belong to the renderer (React 19) process only — presentation-only, no main/preload/IPC involvement.
Renderer UI lives under src/renderer/src/components/; design tokens + mockups under design/.
typecheck = typecheck:node + typecheck:web; build = typecheck && electron-vite build; lint = eslint --cache; no test command exists yet (must be added).
Recommended: Radix primitives + token-styled wrappers (Hybrid) — buy overlay behavior/a11y via Radix (Dialog/DropdownMenu/Popover/Toast), build Icon + styling + Toast queue project-owned.

Four user-facing primitives: Dropdown/popover (anchored, keyboard nav, focus return, click-outside+Escape dismiss, edge-aware positioning), Modal (focus trap, scrim, Escape close, body scroll lock), transient Toast (stacked, auto+manual dismiss, hover-pause), inline SVG Icon.
Shared substrate in src/renderer/src/lib/ — kept THIN given Radix: only what Radix does not provide (Icon helpers, the Toast store, minimal glue), not duplicated focus-trap/positioning.

Design tokens already compiled to CSS custom properties at src/renderer/styles/tokens.css; style via semantic class names bound to those CSS vars, never inline styles. design/reference.html is look/behavior reference only — never reproduce its markup/cruft.
No test runner or component-testing library installed; gating first task adds Vitest + @testing-library/react + user-event (jsdom) PLUS Playwright component tests.
Icon driven by project-owned ~40-icon set (1.5px stroke, 16x16 viewBox); props name (typed union), size, className; unknown-name safe fallback; animations respect prefers-reduced-motion.
Toast fired via imperative API (toast(message,opts)) backed by a zustand toastStore owning the queue; Dropdown + Modal are controlled (open + onOpenChange props), caller owns state.

Edge-aware flip/shift within viewport; nested overlays compose (Escape closes topmost only, focus-trap/return nest); toast stacking + rapid-fire queue + hover/focus pause; trigger unmount while open; unknown icon fallback; prefers-reduced-motion.

Done = all four primitives render per the visual reference and behave correctly, meeting WCAG keyboard/focus expectations, gated by automated interaction tests + Playwright CT, with a dev-only in-app demo route exercising every primitive in its states.
App-internal only (renderer feature components consume it), presentation-only — no network/request-response/persistence; no published-package API-stability concern.
One open uncertainty: keep Icon project-owned vs pulling an icon dependency — discovery resolved it as sound (icon set already exists).
Spec stays behavior-only (WHAT + a11y); Radix recorded as a recommended technical constraint, final library pick deferred to /plan.

decision_points.description: Scope of the shared lib/ substrate given Radix adoption
decision_points.user_answer: Thin lib/, reuse Radix: rely on Radix for focus-trap/positioning/dismiss/ARIA; lib/ holds only Icon helpers, the Toast store, and minimal glue (DRY, search-before-building §6.3).
zustand toastStore owns the queue + imperative toast(msg,opts) API; Radix Toast primitives render each item for a11y/animation.

decision_points.description: Library commitment in the spec: behavior-only with Radix as constraint vs lock Radix in ACs
decision_points.user_answer: Behavior-only ACs (WHAT + a11y); Radix recorded as a recommended technical constraint, final library pick deferred to /plan.
Dev-only in-app route rendering every primitive in its states; doubles as manual-QA surface; no new heavy dependency.
Vitest + @testing-library/react + user-event (jsdom), PLUS Playwright component tests for real-browser focus/keyboard fidelity.

decision_points.description: Nested-overlay composition in v1 vs deferred
decision_points.user_answer: Nested-overlay composition is in v1 scope: modal-opens-dropdown and toast-over-modal compose, Escape closes topmost only, focus-trap/return nest, z-order sane.

decision_points.description: no relevant decision point for existing_behavior
no relevant decision point for breaking_changes

mandatory_reads.n_a_reason: constitution.md has no §7 scaffolding guide (ends at §6 Workflow Rules); renderer placement derived from §2.3 Module Organization + CLAUDE.md project structure + discover integration surface
no .claude/memory/MEMORY.md exists yet (first feature); no prior-feature lessons recorded

overview: A reusable, presentation-only UI primitives layer for the mintEnvoy renderer: a Dropdown/popover, a Modal dialog, a transient Toast, and an inline SVG Icon, plus the thin shared overlay substrate they need. The overlay behavior and accessibility (focus trap/return, keyboard navigation, dismiss, ARIA, edge-aware positioning) are sourced from a headless primitive library (Radix recommended, final pick deferred to /plan); the Icon, the styling, and the Toast queue are project-owned. Every primitive is styled exclusively via semantic class names bound to the existing design-token CSS custom properties, and is consumed only by other renderer feature components — no network, no persistence.
current_state: Greenfield for UI primitives. The renderer is electron-vite boilerplate: src/renderer/src/App.tsx, src/renderer/src/main.tsx, and a single src/renderer/src/components/Versions.tsx. No Dropdown/Modal/Toast/Icon, no overlay hooks, and no src/renderer/src/lib/ exist (confirmed via the codebase-memory graph). Design tokens are already compiled to CSS custom properties at src/renderer/styles/tokens.css, and the @renderer alias (electron.vite.config.ts) resolves to src/renderer/src. No test framework is installed (package.json declares no runner or testing library), and constitution §3.4 requires picking a renderer test stack before the first feature needing coverage. The visual/behavior reference is the Claude Design export under design/ (look only — never its generated markup/cruft).
desired_behavior: Deliver four primitives under the renderer's atomic-design layout — Icon in src/renderer/src/components/atoms/, and Dropdown/popover, Modal, Toast in src/renderer/src/components/molecules/ — with a THIN shared substrate in src/renderer/src/lib/ holding only what the headless library does not provide (Icon helpers, the Toast store, minimal glue). Dropdown/popover anchors to a trigger with keyboard navigation, focus return to the trigger on close, click-outside and Escape dismiss, and edge-aware flip/shift positioning. Modal traps focus, renders a scrim, closes on Escape, and locks body scroll. Toast is fired through an imperative toast(message, opts) API backed by a zustand toastStore that owns a stack of transient notifications with auto-dismiss, manual dismiss, and hover/focus pause. Icon renders an inline 16x16, 1.5px-stroke SVG selected by a typed name from the project-owned 40-icon set, with a safe fallback for an unknown name. Nested overlays compose: Escape closes only the topmost, focus traps and focus-return nest, z-order stays sane. All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion. Dropdown and Modal are controlled via open + onOpenChange props. A dev-only in-app route renders every primitive in its states as the visual + manual-QA surface. Behavior is gated by automated interaction tests (Vitest + Testing Library) plus Playwright component tests for focus/keyboard fidelity.

affected_areas.impact: Create new — inline SVG Icon component + project-owned typed icon-path set (40 icons, 1.5px stroke, 16x16)
Create new — Dropdown/popover, Modal, Toast components wrapping headless behavior, styled via semantic classes
Create new — THIN lib: zustand toastStore + imperative toast() API + minimal glue; relies on the headless lib for focus-trap/positioning
Create new per-component stylesheets using semantic class names bound to existing tokens.css CSS custom properties; no inline styles
Create new — add Vitest + @testing-library/react + user-event (jsdom) and Playwright component tests; record stack in docs/architecture.md
Create new — dev-only in-app route rendering every primitive in its states

acceptance_criteria.statement: The project shall own an inline SVG icon-set module under the atoms directory.
WHEN an open dropdown has focus and the user presses the Arrow, Home, or End keys, the dropdown shall move focus between its menu items.
WHEN a dropdown or modal closes, the component shall return focus to the trigger element that opened it.
WHEN the user clicks outside an open dropdown or presses the Escape key, the dropdown shall dismiss.
WHILE an open dropdown would overflow the viewport, the dropdown shall flip or shift its position to remain fully visible.
WHILE a modal is open, the modal shall confine keyboard focus within its content, render a scrim, and lock body scroll.
WHEN the user presses the Escape key in an open modal, the modal shall close and return focus to its trigger.
WHEN a toast auto-dismiss duration elapses, the toast shall be removed from the stack.
WHILE a toast is hovered or focused, the toast shall pause its auto-dismiss timer.
WHEN the user dismisses one stacked toast, the toast layer shall remove only that toast and leave the others.
WHEN a controlled Dropdown or Modal open prop changes, the component shall reflect the open state and invoke onOpenChange on user-driven changes.
WHEN the Escape key is pressed while nested overlays are open, the system shall close only the topmost overlay.
IF the Icon name is not present in the icon set, THEN the Icon component shall render a safe fallback instead of throwing.
WHILE the prefers-reduced-motion setting is active, the primitives shall suppress their open and close animations.
The exported primitive components and their public prop types shall carry documentation comments.
The renderer source shall pass strict type-checking.
The codebase shall pass ESLint without new errors.
The primitive component sources shall contain no inline style attributes.
The renderer primitive code shall not import the electron or node modules directly.
The build shall include a configured Vitest test runner with Testing Library plus Playwright component tests.
The renderer shall provide the primitives under the atoms and molecules component directories.
WHEN the imperative toast API is invoked, the toastStore shall enqueue a transient notification in the toast stack.
The Icon component shall render a known icon name as an inline SVG using the 16x16 viewBox and stroke width defined by the icon set.
WHEN the renderer test stack is added, the system shall record the chosen test stack in the architecture docs.

ac_subsection_na.behavior_preservation: Greenfield feature — no existing behavior to preserve; renderer has no prior primitives.
ac_subsection_na.ci_pipeline: No CI pipeline changes in scope; tests run via npm scripts, not a new pipeline stage.
ac_subsection_na.hooks_gates: No new git hooks or commit gates introduced by this feature.

out_of_scope.content: Any network / request-response or persistence logic — the primitives are presentation-only.
UI elements beyond the four named primitives (buttons, inputs, tabs, tables, etc.).
A theming engine, runtime theme switcher, or authoring/editing of design token files — primitives only consume existing tokens.
Reproducing the design/reference.html markup or its generated cruft (data-om-*, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, tweaks-panel).
Published-package API-stability guarantees — the layer is app-internal only.

constraints.content: strict is on; do not weaken it per-file. No 'any' — type external input as 'unknown' and narrow with a guard. The Icon name must be a typed string-literal union.
Renderer modules resolve through the @renderer alias; import via the alias, not deep relative paths. Keep code in its process dir — no cross-process imports.
Pick a renderer test stack (Vitest + Testing Library) before the first feature that needs coverage; record it in docs/architecture.md.
Components PascalCase; hooks camelCase use-prefixed; zustand stores Store-suffixed.
Never swallow errors — no empty catch; handle both success and error paths of every fallible operation.
Shared renderer state (the Toast queue) lives in a zustand toastStore; never mutate store state outside its actions (constitution §4 Always-Do).

Primitives are renderer-only and presentation-only — no main/preload/IPC involvement; never import node or electron in renderer code (constitution §4). Primitives live under src/renderer/src/components and the styling consumes tokens under design/.

Style exclusively via semantic class names bound to the existing tokens.css CSS custom properties; no inline styles. design/reference.html is a look/behavior reference only — never reproduce its markup.
Radix is the recommended headless library for the overlay behavior; this spec stays behavior-only and the final library pick is deferred to /plan.
Keep the lib/ substrate thin: do not hand-roll focus-trap or positioning that the headless library already provides (constitution §6.3 search-before-building).
Meet WCAG 2.1 AA keyboard and focus expectations for every primitive (focus trap/return, Escape, ARIA roles).
The Icon stays project-owned — no icon-library dependency is introduced (the SVG path set already exists).

open_questions.content: Final headless-library selection (Radix vs Base UI vs React Aria) is deferred to /plan; discovery recommends Radix.
Confirm Icon stays project-owned vs pulling an icon dependency — discovery resolved this as sound since the ~40 SVG paths already exist.

risks.risk: Radix Toast may not natively cover stacking + hover/focus pause + rapid-fire queue.
risks.mitigation: Derisk spike; if it falls short, the zustand toastStore owns the queue and Radix Toast renders individual items.

risks.risk: No test framework exists, so the Vitest + Playwright setup is a gating prerequisite that could slip and block all interaction-test ACs.
risks.mitigation: Make the test-stack setup the first task; record the stack in docs/architecture.md per constitution §3.4.

risks.risk: Nested-overlay composition (Escape topmost-only, nested focus-trap/return) is the most failure-prone behavior.
risks.mitigation: Prototype nested behavior early; cover with dedicated interaction + Playwright tests.

risks.risk: Styling Radix component state (open/closed, side) via semantic classes may fight the token-driven stylesheet approach.
risks.mitigation: Spike a Radix Dialog + DropdownMenu styled solely via token CSS vars before broad rollout.

risks.risk: jsdom may not faithfully reproduce focus/keyboard behavior, hiding real a11y defects.
risks.mitigation: Run Playwright component tests in a real browser for focus/keyboard fidelity.

approval_summary:
I've created the specification at `specs/001-ui-primitives/spec.md`. Key points:
- **What changes**: A reusable, presentation-only UI primitives layer for the mintEnvoy renderer: a Dropdown/popover, a Modal dialog, a transient Toast, and an inline SVG Icon, plus the thin shared overlay substrate they need. The overlay behavior and acces...
- **Files affected**: 14 files across 6 areas
- **Acceptance criteria**: 24 testable criteria across 4 AC categories
- **Out of scope**: Any network / request-response or persistence logic — the primitives are present; UI elements beyond the four named primitives (buttons, inputs, tabs, tables, etc; A theming engine, runtime theme switcher, or authoring/editing of design token f; …

Please review and either approve or request changes. Once approved, run `/plan` to create the technical implementation plan.
plan_handoff_block:
## Manual next step — run /plan

A structured handoff (specs/001-ui-primitives/handoff.json) is written for /plan. /plan auto-discovers it on its first run and reads the upstream plan-seeds — but you still launch /plan manually (there is no auto-dispatch from /specify). Restart Claude Code (exit and relaunch the CLI/app so the newly installed command is picked up), then run the command below in this repo. The spec path is explicit so /plan does not need most-recent-spec discovery:

~~~
/plan specs/001-ui-primitives/spec.md
~~~

Minimum handoff data:
- Spec status: Draft
- Spec type: greenfield_feature
- AC count: 24 across 4 subsections (5.1: 3, 5.2: 0, 5.3: 15, 5.4: 0, 5.5: 0, 5.6: 2, 5.7: 4)
- Decision-point coverage: 6 answered, 0 default-applied, 0 deferred-OOS, 0 deferred-open-question
- Affected areas: 6 across src
- Out-of-scope items: 5
- Open questions: 2
- Constraints: 12
- Risks: 5
- Phase 1.5 finding coverage: 100% (all findings landed)

Reference: specs/001-ui-primitives/spec.md

open_question_resolutions.resolution_text: Plan selects Radix UI (unified radix-ui package) as the headless library; alternatives rejected per research.md.
Icon stays project-owned (typed name union over existing SVG paths); no icon-library dependency.
